evidence_quotes.quote: The Stop Destroying Videogames initiative seeks to ensure that games publishers of video games with online dependencies do not render them unplayable.
The example is that a digital game is sold as a good to a given customer, with no indication of when support ends, no means of continued usage of the game upon shutdown of the server and no refund.

Knowing that publishers can and do take away my ability to play games purchased digitally or physically has me convinced that stopping this from happening would be well in line with the Digital Fairness Act's goals.

paragraphs.text: I strongly approve of this Act, first off. The section regarding chatbots is especially resonant. I would like to bring to your attention an initiative that I feel aligns very well with this act's intentions. The Stop Destroying Videogames initiative seeks to ensure that games publishers of video games with online dependencies do not render them unplayable. The example is that a digital game is sold as a good to a given customer, with no indication of when support ends, no means of continued usage of the game upon shutdown of the server and no refund. For the sake of media preservation it would mean a great deal to see some effort to rectify this unfairness. Gaming has been a significant positive factor in my life from childhood onward. Knowing that publishers can and do take away my ability to play games purchased digitally or physically has me convinced that stopping this from happening would be well in line with the Digital Fairness Act's goals.
